steps.title: Join the example correctly
steps.content: The blank comes between two independent clauses: "many are still well known" and "Malika Ouattara, for example, is a celebrated slam poet from Burkina Faso." The second clause gives an example of the first claim, so the punctuation must join two complete clauses and introduce an example.

steps.title: Remove boundary errors
steps.content: A creates a comma splice by placing only a comma between two independent clauses. C uses "and" without the comma needed before a coordinating conjunction joining independent clauses, and it weakens the example relationship. D creates a run-on sentence with no punctuation between the clauses. The answer is B.
